title: Convergence diagnostics
text: TMLE update tries make empirical mean target EIC component small. original stopping rule checks: relative, component-specific threshold. can become extremely strict event rare component near-zero variability. concrete now exposes three stopping rules: setting EICStopRule = "hybrid" EICStopAbsTol = 1e-3 means: keep original relative criterion unless stricter absolute empirical EIC tolerance 0.001 risk scale.
code: abs(PnEIC) <= seEIC / (sqrt(n) * log(n))

title: Convergence diagnostics
text: component table usually useful first view. Key columns: PnEIC: empirical mean EIC component RelativeCriteria: original relative stopping threshold AbsoluteCriteria: absolute threshold supplied EICStopAbsTol StopCriteria: threshold used selected rule ratio: abs(PnEIC) / StopCriteria check: whether component passed selected rule Converged: whether overall update converged ConvergenceStep: update step convergence reached Focus first rows check == FALSE, sorted ratio.
code: components <- getTmleDiagnostics(ConcreteEst, type = "components") components[order(ratio, decreasing = TRUE)]  trace <- getTmleDiagnostics(ConcreteEst, type = "trace") trace  norm <- getTmleDiagnostics(ConcreteEst, type = "norm") norm components[check == FALSE][order(ratio, decreasing = TRUE)]

title: concrete
text: concrete R package designed use targeted maximum likelihood estimation (TMLE) compute covariate-adjusted marginal cumulative incidence estimates right-censored survival settings without competing risks. concrete intended help trial analysts estimate clinically interpretable absolute risks, risk differences, risk ratios prespecified follow-times using flexible nuisance estimation TMLE diagnostics.

title: concrete
text: testing concrete randomized trial trial-like data set, start articles: Trialist quickstart: required data layout, first intent--treat analysis, output interpretation, checks compare standard trial summaries. Learner library guide: conservative Cox-analyses, treatment Super Learner libraries, hazard learner options Coxnet, random survival forests, additive hazards, HAL. Convergence diagnostics: inspect empirical EIC diagnostics try TMLE update converge cleanly. Testing protocol current limitations: built-smoke test, conservative--flexible testing ladder, current scope supported trial data structures. minimum data columns subject id, observed time, event type, binary treatment arm, baseline covariates. Censoring coded 0; event types positive integers. competing risks, use one positive integer event interest positive integers competing events. check installation works using data, run installed smoke test: also try optional learners installed locally:

title: formatArguments — formatArguments
text: DataTable data.table (n x (d + (3:5)); data.table observed data, rows n = number observations d = number baseline covariates. DataTable must include following columns: "EventTime": numeric; real numbers > 0, observed event censoring time "EventType": numeric; observed event type, censoring events indicated integers <= 0 "Treatment": numeric; observed treatment value. Binary treatments must coded 0, 1 "Treatment": numeric; observed treatment May include "ID": factor, character, numeric; unique subject id. ID column missing, row numbers used ID. longitudinal data, ID must provided "Baseline Covariates": factor, character, numeric; EventTime character: column name observed event censoring time EventType character: column name observed event type. (0 indicating censoring) Treatment character: column name observed treatment assignment ID character (default: NULL): column name observed subject id longitudinal data structures TargetTime numeric: vector target times. NULL, last observed non-censoring event time targeted. TargetEvent numeric: vector target events - subset unique EventTypes. NULL, non-censoring observed event types targeted. Intervention list: list desired interventions treatment variable. intervention must list containing two named functions: 'intervention' = function(treatment vector, covariate data) 'gstar' = function(treatment vector, covariate data) concrete::makeITT() can used specify intent--treat analysis binary intervention variable CVArg list: arguments passed .call(origami::make_folds). NULL, default list(n = nrow(DataTable), fold_fun = folds_vfold, cluster_ids = NULL, strata_ids = NULL) Model list (default: NULL): named list models, one failure censoring event one Treatment variable. Treatment models passed SuperLearner. Hazard models can Cox formulas one-word learner aliases. Supported hazard aliases "coxnet", "rsf"/"randomForestSRC", "aareg"/"additive_hazards", "hal"/"hal9001". optional learners require glmnet, randomForestSRC, hal9001 selected. Model = NULL, Cox model template generated user amend. MaxUpdateIter numeric (default: 500): number one-step update steps OneStepEps numeric (default: 0.1): one-step TMLE step size MinNuisance numeric (default: 5/log(n)/sqrt(n)): value (0, 1) truncating g-related denominator clever covariate Verbose boolean GComp boolean (default: TRUE): return g-computation formula plug-estimates ReturnModels boolean (default: TRUE): return fitted models initial estimation stage ConcreteArgs list (default: NULL, yet ready) : Use recheck amended output previous formatArguments() calls. non-NULL input cause arguments ignored. RenameCovs boolean (default: TRUE): whether rename covariates UpdateMethod character (default: "standard"): TMLE update method. Supported values "standard", "adaptive", "coordinated". "adaptive" uses line search rollback usually stable choice difficult convergence cases. "accelerated" accepted legacy alias "adaptive". EICStopRule character (default: "relative"): TMLE stopping rule. Supported values "relative", "absolute", "hybrid". "relative" preserves original criterion, |PnEIC| <= seEIC / (sqrt(n) log(n)). "absolute" checks |PnEIC| <= EICStopAbsTol. "hybrid" checks |PnEIC| <= max(seEIC / (sqrt(n) log(n)), EICStopAbsTol), useful rare-event near-zero-variance components make relative criterion numerically strict. EICStopAbsTol numeric (default: 0): absolute |PnEIC| tolerance used EICStopRule "absolute" "hybrid". value 1e-3 gives small absolute-risk scale tolerance default 0 leaves original relative rule unchanged. ... additional arguments passed print methods x ConcreteArgs object

title: formatArguments — formatArguments
text: list class "ConcreteArgs" Data: data.table containing EventTime, EventType, Treatment, potentially ID baseline covariates. following attributes EventTime: column name observed event censoring time EventType: column name observed event type. (0 indicating censoring) Treatment: column name observed treatment assignment ID: column name observed subject id RenameCovs: boolean whether covariates renamed TargetTime: numeric vector target times evaluate risk/survival TargetEvent: numeric vector target events Regime: named list desired regimes, tagged 'g.star' attribute function Regime[[]]: vector desired treatment assignments attr(Regime[[]], "g.star"): function Treatment Covariates, outputting vector desired treatment assignment probabilities CVFolds: list cross-validation fold assignments structure output origami::make_folds() Model: named list model specifications, one unique 'EventType' one 'Treatment' variable. MaxUpdateIter: number one-step update steps OneStepEps: initial one-step TMLE step size MinNuisance: numeric lower bound propensity score denominator efficient influence function Verbose: boolean print additional information GComp: boolean return g-computation formula plug-estimates ReturnModels: boolean return fitted models initial estimation stage EICStopRule: TMLE stopping rule empirical mean EIC checks EICStopAbsTol: absolute empirical mean EIC tolerance absolute hybrid stopping

makeITT() creates a list of functions to specify intent-to-treat #   regimes for a binary, single, point treatment variable intervention <- makeITT()  # formatArguments() returns correctly formatted arguments for doConcrete() #   If no input is provided for the Model argument, a default will be generated concrete.args <- formatArguments(DataTable = data,                                  EventTime = "time",                                  EventType = "status",                                  Treatment = "trt",                                  ID = "id",                                  TargetTime = 2500,                                  TargetEvent = c(1, 2),                                  Intervention = intervention,                                  CVArg = list(V = 2))  # Alternatively, estimation algorithms can be provided as a named list model <- list("trt" = c("SL.glm", "SL.glmnet"),               "0" = list(Surv(time, status == 0) ~ .),               "1" = list(Surv(time, status == 1) ~ .),               "2" = list(Surv(time, status == 2) ~ .)) concrete.args <- formatArguments(DataTable = data,                                  EventTime = "time",                                  EventType = "status",                                  Treatment = "trt",                                  ID = "id",                                  TargetTime = 2500,                                  TargetEvent = c(1, 2),                                  Intervention = intervention,                                  CVArg = list(V = 2),                                  Model = model)  # title: concrete 1.1.0
text: Added user-facing TMLE convergence controls formatArguments(): UpdateMethod, EICStopRule, EICStopAbsTol. Added hybrid absolute empirical EIC stopping rules. default remains original relative rule. Added getTmleDiagnostics() inspecting final component-wise empirical EIC diagnostics, update traces, norm trajectories fitted ConcreteEst objects. Added documented hazard learner aliases Coxnet, random survival forests, additive hazards, HAL. Added trialist-facing documentation, pkgdown site, GitHub issue templates, installed smoke-test script checking Cox-optional learner workflows.

title: concrete 1.1.0
text: Added trace fields relative absolute EIC ratios, maximum absolute empirical EIC, failing components, stopping-rule metadata. Improved rare-event convergence behavior hybrid stopping rule preserving original default behavior.
